bullet_points: Load the dataset and perform log-transformations on all level variables (gdp_per_capita, capital_stock, labor_force, human_capital, tfp).
Conduct Levin-Lin-Chu (LLC) unit root tests on the transformed series to assess stationarity.
Standardize and mean-center the policy variables (trade_openness, government_expenditure_share).
Save the cleaned, log-transformed, and standardized dataset as processed_panel.csv in the workspace for use in subsequent steps.
Implement the System GMM estimator to model the investment rate as a function of lagged investment, GDP per capita, and policy variables.
Perform diagnostic tests (Arellano-Bond AR(2), Hansen J-test, Kleibergen-Paap F-statistic) to validate the GMM specification.
Save the estimated coefficients and diagnostic statistics to a results file.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Data Pre-processing, Stationarity Diagnostics, and System GMM Estimation

bullet_points: Load the processed_panel.csv file generated in Step 1.
Estimate the production function parameters and the depreciation rate (δ) using a constrained optimization framework.
Perform a sensitivity analysis on the δ estimation by calculating and reporting standard errors or confidence intervals to quantify parameter uncertainty.
Calculate the target steady-state capital stock (K*) for each country-year observation based on the predicted investment rate from Step 1 and the estimated δ.
Compute the "distance to steady-state" metric (Di,t).
Estimate the moderated transition model using the instrumented interaction terms between Di,t and policy variables.
Generate a multi-panel plot showing the marginal effect of the distance to steady-state on capital growth at different percentiles of policy variables.
Save the final model outputs, sensitivity analysis results, and the plot to the workspace.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Structural Parameter Estimation and Transition Gap Analysis

bullet_points: Review the diagnostic statistics, structural parameter estimates, and sensitivity analysis results generated in the previous steps.

Compare the estimated capital elasticity from the production function against the theoretical benchmark of 0.35, discussing deviations as potential evidence of measurement noise or model misspecification.
Interpret the elasticity of the investment rate with respect to GDP per capita and the role of policy variables as moderators of transition speed.
Discuss the implications of the findings regarding the structural feedback loop between income levels and capital accumulation.
Synthesize the results into a comprehensive report, referencing the key statistics and plots to support the conclusions on how institutional factors influence growth trajectories.
